name: Baihu
description: Baihu, also known as the White Tiger, is one of the Four Symbols of the Chinese constellations. It represents the West and the autumn season. Associated with the element of metal, Baihu is a powerful and fearsome creature, often depicted with gleaming white fur and piercing eyes. It symbolizes courage, righteousness, and military prowess, and is believed to ward off evil spirits and bring good fortune. In some traditions, Baihu is considered a deity, ruling over the western quadrant of the heavens. Temples dedicated to Baihu are often visited by people seeking protection and blessings. Its image has been used throughout Chinese history in art, literature, and military emblems, signifying strength and authority. The White Tiger is a guardian spirit, ensuring justice and order in the world. Its roar is said to shake the earth and its presence commands respect and awe. Though fearsome in appearance, Baihu is ultimately a benevolent protector, safeguarding those who are righteous and just from harm.
subtitle: Guardian of the West, Autumn's Fury
appearance: Baihu is a magnificent tiger with pure white fur, often described as being as white as snow. Its eyes are piercing and glow with an inner light, said to be able to see through deception and evil. Its claws and teeth are sharp and formidable, capable of rending flesh and bone. Its size is often depicted as larger than an ordinary tiger, befitting its status as a celestial being. Some depictions include metallic markings on its fur, representing its association with the metal element. It exudes an aura of power and authority, commanding respect from all who behold it.
habitat: Baihu is believed to reside in the western mountains and forests of China, particularly in regions associated with metal deposits. It is also considered a celestial being, inhabiting the western quadrant of the heavens. Temples dedicated to Baihu are often located in mountainous areas or near sites of historical significance, reflecting its connection to the land and its role as a guardian spirit.
behavior: Baihu is a solitary creature, preferring to roam its territory alone. It is fiercely protective of its domain and will defend it against any perceived threat. While fearsome in appearance, Baihu is ultimately a benevolent guardian, only unleashing its power against those who are evil or unjust. It is said to be a keen observer of human behavior, rewarding those who are righteous and punishing those who are wicked. Baihu is also associated with the autumn season, symbolizing a time of reflection and judgment.

keyMyths.title: Guardian of the West
keyMyths.description: Baihu is one of the Four Symbols, each representing a cardinal direction. As the guardian of the West, it protects the land from evil spirits and ensures the balance of the cosmos.

keyMyths.title: Symbol of Autumn
keyMyths.description: Associated with the autumn season, Baihu represents harvest, justice, and the element of metal. Its presence signifies a time of reflection and judgment, rewarding the righteous and punishing the wicked.

sources.author: Sima Qian
sources.description: A comprehensive historical account of China, mentioning the Four Symbols.
sources.text: A comprehensive historical account of China, mentioning the Four Symbols.

sources.author: Various
sources.description: An ancient geographical and mythical text that may contain early references to tiger deities.
